title: Bridge Essay: The Impact of Outdoor in Nature Mindfulness on the Mental Well-being of Children and Adolescents. A Mental Health and Cross-cultural Approach.
concept: Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.

**The Impact of Outdoor in Nature Mindfulness on the Mental Well-being of Children and Adolescents. A Mental Health and Cross-cultural Approach.** - Mater Sociomed (2024). DOI: 10.5455/msm.2024.36.73-76. PMID: 38590591. BACKGROUND: Mindfulness, originating from ancient meditation practices, has garnered substantial scientific interest in recent decades due to its potential to enhance various aspects of well-being. The rise of scientific interest in mindfulness has led to the development of Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) and other mindfulness-based interventions (MBIs), which have demonstrated positive effects on stress reduction, emotional regulation, and cognitive flexibility. OBJECTIVE: This study
